mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and increases the relative attractiveness of equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if lower 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than rising FEDFUNDS periods in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, labor-market slack signals weaker household income growth and higher earnings risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or no return underperformance versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Positive RSAFS momentum combined with falling UNRATE identifies broadening household demand and improving income security, raising expected revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-up and UNRATE-down condition fails to outperform either single-series signal over rolling 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trend in INDPRO signals rising factory utilization and stronger goods-sector earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum has a nonpositive average 60-day S&P 500 return or Rank IC below 0.03 in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses below 1100 thousand units, weakening residential construction signals softer cyclical demand and tighter credit-sensitive activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-threshold triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains near zero creates an inflation-pressure gap that raises expected future tightening and discount-rate risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-pressure gap has positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or does not underperform low-inflation zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth signals expanding real aggregate demand and stronger corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive year-over-year GDPC1 growth does not generate higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than nonpositive GDPC1 growth in walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25%, abundant short-term liquidity and suppressed cash yields force portfolio reallocation toward equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS-below-0.25% triggers do not produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than the unconditional hit rate.
